channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Young scientists and innovators around the world now have an exceptional opportunity to gain international recognition for their contributions to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). UNESCO has officially launched the call for nominations and applications for the third edition of the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM. This […]

The post UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young scientists and innovators around the world now have an exceptional opportunity to gain international recognition for their contributions to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). UNESCO has officially launched the call for nominations and applications for the third edition of the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM.

This globally recognized biennial prize celebrates outstanding young researchers, innovators, educators, and scientific leaders whose work contributes to advancing scientific knowledge, strengthening STEM education, promoting innovation, and fostering international cooperation in support of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Five outstanding laureates from different regions of the world will each receive a monetary award of USD 50,000, in addition to a medal and diploma recognizing their achievements and contributions to society.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1)

Applicants and nominators are strongly encouraged to begin the application process early to allow sufficient time for account verification, nomination submission, and document preparation.

About the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM was established to strengthen scientific research, improve STEM education, encourage innovation, and enhance international scientific cooperation.

The Prize supports UNESCO’s broader mission of promoting science as a driver of sustainable development and recognizes young scientists whose work addresses some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including climate change, sustainable development, technological innovation, education, public health, environmental sustainability, and socio-economic advancement.

Through this initiative, UNESCO seeks to inspire the next generation of scientists while encouraging governments, institutions, and communities to invest in scientific excellence and innovation.

The Prize also places a strong emphasis on gender equality by encouraging the participation and recognition of women and girls in STEM disciplines.

Prize Benefits

Selected laureates will receive:

Financial Award

  • USD 50,000 for each laureate

International Recognition

  • UNESCO international recognition
  • Global visibility among scientific communities
  • Increased opportunities for collaboration and networking
  • Enhanced professional credibility

Official Honors

  • UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Medal
  • UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Diploma

Career Advancement Opportunities

  • Increased exposure to global scientific networks
  • Opportunities for international partnerships
  • Recognition from leading scientific institutions worldwide

Number of Awards Available

The Prize is awarded to five laureates every two years, representing the following geographical regions:

  1. Africa
  2. Arab States
  3. Asia and the Pacific
  4. Europe and North America
  5. Latin America and the Caribbean

This regional representation ensures global inclusion and recognizes scientific excellence across diverse communities and research environments.

Objectives of the Prize

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize aims to:

  • Promote excellence in STEM research
  • Encourage innovation and scientific discovery
  • Support STEM education and knowledge dissemination
  • Strengthen international scientific cooperation
  • Advance progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals
  • Increase participation of women and girls in STEM
  • Inspire future generations of scientists and innovators

Who Can Apply?

The Prize is open to young scientists, researchers, innovators, educators, and STEM professionals who have demonstrated exceptional contributions in their respective fields.

Candidates must have achieved significant recognition for their work and demonstrate measurable impact within their area of expertise.

Eligibility Criteria

Age Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be under 40 years of age on the application deadline date (30 November 2026)

Achievement Requirement

Candidates must have demonstrated significant achievements or contributions in one or more of the following areas:

1. STEM Research

Including:

  • Scientific research
  • Applied research
  • Emerging technologies
  • Breakthrough discoveries
  • Innovative scientific solutions

2. STEM Education and Innovation

Including:

  • STEM teaching and learning initiatives
  • Science communication
  • Dissemination of research findings
  • Educational innovation
  • Creativity and technological innovation

3. International or Regional Cooperation

Including:

  • Cross-border scientific collaboration
  • Regional research partnerships
  • International STEM initiatives
  • Scientific diplomacy
  • Knowledge-sharing programmes

Focus on Gender Equality

UNESCO strongly encourages nominations of young women scientists.

The Prize seeks to address gender disparities in STEM by recognizing female scientific leaders and inspiring more girls and young women to pursue careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

Application Process

Applicants must complete an online application dossier.

Step 1: Create an Online Account

Candidates must:

  • Register online
  • Verify their email address
  • Complete their profile information

Step 2: Complete the Application Dossier

Applicants must provide detailed information about:

  • Educational background
  • Professional achievements
  • Scientific contributions
  • Research impact
  • Innovation activities
  • International collaboration experience

Step 3: Upload Supporting Documents

Required documents include:

  • Description of background and achievements
  • Summary of research work and publications
  • Evidence of significant contributions
  • Statement explaining contribution to the Prize objectives
  • Additional supporting documentation

Step 4: Secure a Valid Nomination

Applications are only valid if accompanied by an eligible nomination.

Candidates must ensure their nominator submits the nomination before the application deadline.

Step 5: Submit Before the Deadline

Both the application and nomination must be completed by:

30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1)

Nomination Requirements

A nomination is mandatory.

Applications submitted without an eligible nomination will not be considered.

Who Can Nominate?

Eligible nominators include:

Government Institutions

  • Governments of UNESCO Member States
  • National Commissions for UNESCO

UNESCO Partners

  • UNESCO Chairs
  • Category 2 Institutes and Centres under UNESCO

Scientific Organizations

  • International scientific unions
  • Relevant scientific bodies

Non-Governmental Organizations

  • NGOs maintaining official partnerships with UNESCO
  • Organizations active in STEM-related fields

Universities

Eligible university representatives include:

  • Department Heads
  • Deans
  • University management officials

Individual faculty members and staff members are not eligible to submit nominations independently.

What Must a Nomination Include?

A nomination should contain:

Candidate Background

  • Professional profile
  • Educational background
  • Major achievements

Scientific Contributions

  • Publications
  • Research outputs
  • Innovation achievements
  • Major accomplishments

Contribution to Prize Objectives

  • Impact on STEM advancement
  • Educational contributions
  • Innovation and scientific leadership
  • International cooperation efforts

Important Application Notes

Applicants should carefully note the following:

  • Self-nominations alone are not accepted
  • Applications must be completed in English or French
  • Nominations must be completed in English or French
  • Incomplete submissions will be rejected
  • Late applications will not be accepted
  • Account verification may require additional time
  • Each nominating institution may nominate up to three candidates
  • Supporting documents must be submitted before the deadline

Why This Prize Matters

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize is one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions for emerging scientific leaders.

Beyond the financial award, the Prize provides recipients with international visibility, global recognition, and access to influential scientific networks that can accelerate their careers and amplify the impact of their work.

For young researchers and innovators dedicated to solving global challenges through science and technology, this Prize represents a powerful platform for showcasing excellence and contributing to sustainable development worldwide.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Interested candidates should begin preparing their applications and securing eligible nominators as early as possible to ensure successful submission before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM offers an extraordinary opportunity for young scientists, researchers, innovators, and educators to gain international recognition for their contributions to science and society.

With five awards of USD 50,000 each, global visibility, and recognition from UNESCO, the Prize celebrates the next generation of scientific leaders working to advance knowledge, innovation, education, and sustainable development around the world.

Eligible candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications and nominations well ahead of the 30 November 2026 deadline.

Click here for more global award opportunities

 

The post UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) has announced multiple international career opportunities for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, climate action, economic transformation, regional cooperation, and public administration across the Asia-Pacific region. These positions span senior leadership roles, programme management opportunities, and specialized consultancy assignments. Successful […]

The post United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) has announced multiple international career opportunities for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, climate action, economic transformation, regional cooperation, and public administration across the Asia-Pacific region.

These positions span senior leadership roles, programme management opportunities, and specialized consultancy assignments. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to work with one of the most influential regional bodies of the United Nations while helping member states achieve sustainable development goals and long-term economic resilience.

Applications are currently open for four positions, with deadlines ranging from 8 June to 4 July 2026.

About ESCAP

The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) serves as the regional development arm of the United Nations Secretariat.

ESCAP works to:

  • Promote sustainable and inclusive economic growth
  • Strengthen regional connectivity
  • Support climate action initiatives
  • Enhance policy development and implementation
  • Facilitate technical cooperation among member states
  • Advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Through policy research, technical assistance, capacity-building programmes, and regional cooperation initiatives, ESCAP supports countries in addressing emerging economic, environmental, and social challenges.

1. Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1)

Location

Almaty, Kazakhstan

Application Deadline

27 June 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is seeking an experienced international development leader to head its Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (SONCA).

The selected candidate will oversee ESCAP’s engagement with:

  • Armenia
  • Azerbaijan
  • Georgia
  • Kazakhstan
  • Kyrgyzstan
  • Russian Federation
  • Tajikistan
  • Turkmenistan
  • Uzbekistan

As the organization’s senior representative in the subregion, the successful candidate will lead policy dialogue, regional cooperation initiatives, stakeholder engagement, and strategic programme implementation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Represent ESCAP throughout North and Central Asia
  • Lead subregional development programmes
  • Support implementation of the Special Programme for Economies of Central Asia (SPECA)
  • Provide policy advice to senior UN leadership
  • Build partnerships with governments, development partners, civil society, and academia
  • Manage budgets, staffing, and programme delivery
  • Support regional sustainable development initiatives

Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Master’s degree in Economics, Social Sciences, or a related field
  • Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible professional experience
  • Significant international-level experience
  • Strong policy formulation and programme management expertise
  • Experience working with governments and international organizations

Why Apply?

This role offers a unique opportunity to influence development policy and regional cooperation across one of the world’s most strategically important regions.

Click here for more and to apply

2. Programme Officer (P-3)

Location

Bangkok, Thailand

Application Deadline

23 June 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is recruiting a Programme Officer to support the implementation of the Energy Transition for Green Growth and Prosperity Programme.

This multi-year initiative focuses on accelerating sustainable energy transitions, promoting green industrial development, and supporting economic transformation throughout Asia-Pacific countries.

The successful candidate will work within the Financing for Development Section of the Macroeconomic Policy and Financing for Development Division.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support programme implementation and evaluation
  • Coordinate technical assistance projects
  • Conduct policy research and analysis
  • Support high-level advisory groups and donor engagement
  • Organize workshops and stakeholder consultations
  • Prepare reports, studies, and policy papers
  • Contribute to financing and energy transition initiatives
  • Support regional cooperation programmes

Areas of Focus

The role will contribute to work related to:

  • Energy transition financing
  • Green growth
  • Sustainable development
  • Regional cooperation
  • Power grid interconnection
  • Social development
  • Artificial intelligence and economic transformation
  • Macroeconomic policy

Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, Energy, International Relations, Business Administration, Political Science, or related fields
  • Minimum five years of relevant professional experience
  • Experience in financing energy transition projects
  • Experience supporting international development initiatives
  • Strong research and analytical capabilities

Why Apply?

This is an excellent opportunity for mid-career professionals interested in sustainable finance, energy transition, and international development.

Click here for more and to apply

 

3. Director of Administration (D-1)

Location

Bangkok, Thailand

Application Deadline

04 July 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is seeking a highly experienced executive leader to serve as Director of Administration.

The position oversees all major administrative operations supporting ESCAP and various UN entities across the Asia-Pacific region.

This is one of the most senior administrative leadership positions within ESCAP.

Key Responsibilities

The Director will lead operations covering:

  • Human resources management
  • Finance and budgeting
  • Procurement
  • Information technology services
  • Conference services
  • Real estate management
  • Central support services
  • Travel and transport services
  • Host country relations

The role also includes supporting major UN reform initiatives, modernization programmes, operational efficiency projects, and organizational transformation efforts.

Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • Master’s degree in Public Administration, Business Administration, Finance, Human Resources, Management, or related fields
  • Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible administrative experience
  • Experience leading large multi-functional teams
  • Strong knowledge of UN systems and procedures
  • Expertise in organizational transformation and operational management

Why Apply?

This position offers an opportunity to shape administrative excellence and operational innovation across one of the UN’s largest regional commissions.

Click here for more and to apply

4. Expert in Transport Finance (Consultant)

Duty Station

Bangkok, Thailand

Work Location

Fiji

Application Deadline

08 June 2026

Contract Duration

22 June 2026 – 31 December 2026

Position Overview

ESCAP is recruiting a national consultant to support the development of Fiji’s Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) 3.0 Costed Implementation Plan (CIP).

The consultancy forms part of ESCAP’s regional project aimed at strengthening member states’ capacity to implement low-carbon and climate-resilient transport systems.

The assignment will help transform Fiji’s climate commitments into actionable and financially viable transport investments.

Project Goals

The consultant will contribute to:

  • Low-carbon transport development
  • Climate-resilient transport planning
  • Sustainable transport financing
  • Climate mitigation strategies
  • Green transport investment pipelines
  • NDC implementation

Key Responsibilities

Sector Analysis

  • Analyze Fiji’s transport mitigation targets
  • Review transport decarbonization strategies
  • Assess policy priorities

Project Pipeline Development

  • Identify transport mitigation projects
  • Estimate CAPEX and OPEX requirements
  • Assess investment gaps
  • Prioritize climate investments

Policy and Technical Support

  • Draft transport chapters for the Costed Implementation Plan
  • Develop monitoring and reporting frameworks
  • Integrate climate and gender considerations

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Participate in consultations and workshops
  • Present technical findings
  • Support validation processes

Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

  • Advanced degree in Transport, Civil Engineering, Environmental Science, Climate Change, Energy Studies, or related fields
  • At least seven years of experience in:
    • Sustainable transport
    • Climate change mitigation
    • Transport decarbonization
    • Transport finance
    • Transport policy analysis
  • Knowledge of Fiji’s climate policies and institutional framework
  • Strong expertise in transport modelling and GHG accounting

Why Apply?

This consultancy provides a unique opportunity to directly contribute to Fiji’s climate transition strategy and support sustainable transport investments with long-term environmental and economic impact.

Click here for more and to apply

 

Important Application Deadlines

Applicants should carefully note the following deadlines:

  1. Expert in Transport Finance – 08 June 2026
  2. Programme Officer (P-3) – 23 June 2026
  3. Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1) – 27 June 2026
  4. Director of Administration (D-1) – 04 July 2026

Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to submit applications well before the closing dates.

Why Consider a Career with ESCAP?

Working with ESCAP provides professionals with the opportunity to:

  • Influence regional and global development policy
  • Collaborate with governments and international organizations
  • Support climate action and sustainable development initiatives
  • Build experience within the United Nations system
  • Contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals
  • Work in diverse multicultural environments
  • Lead impactful projects affecting millions of people across Asia and the Pacific

For professionals seeking meaningful international careers, these ESCAP vacancies represent exceptional opportunities to create lasting impact while advancing their careers within the United Nations system.

 

For more global job opportunities click here

 

The post United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI. The programme is […]

The post Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI.

The programme is seeking 3–4 fellows for its current cohort and offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world’s leading centres for AI ethics research.

About the Accelerator Fellowship Programme

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative of the Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI. The programme was created in response to the growing ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and adoption of artificial intelligence technologies across society.

Unlike traditional research fellowships that focus primarily on academic outputs, this programme emphasizes real-world impact. It seeks to support projects that can shape the development of AI in ethical and socially beneficial directions while extending the influence of research already taking place within the Institute.

The fellowship is grounded in:

  • Philosophical inquiry
  • Academic independence
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Practical impact
  • Responsible innovation

Through this approach, Oxford aims to bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI ethics research and meaningful implementation in society.

Fellowship Objectives

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme supports work that:

  • Creates significant impact beyond academia
  • Promotes the responsible and ethical development of AI
  • Aligns with one or more research priorities of the Institute for Ethics in AI
  • Amplifies and extends the impact of existing research at the Institute

The programme encourages fellows to transform research insights into practical initiatives capable of influencing decision-making, industry practices, public understanding, and governance frameworks.

Types of Projects Supported

Oxford’s Accelerator Fellowship Programme welcomes a diverse range of initiatives focused on ethical AI. Potential areas of impact include, but are not limited to:

1. AI Policy and Governance Innovation

Projects that contribute to:

  • AI regulation
  • Governance frameworks
  • Public policy development
  • Ethical standards and accountability systems
  • International cooperation on AI governance

2. Professional Development and Capacity Building

Initiatives designed to:

  • Train AI professionals in ethical practices
  • Create educational opportunities
  • Develop responsible AI leadership
  • Build ethics competencies across sectors

3. Commercial and Technical Innovation

Projects that support:

  • Responsible AI products
  • Ethical AI tools and systems
  • Industry best practices
  • Commercial solutions that prioritize fairness, transparency, and accountability

4. Strategic Networks and Partnerships

Efforts focused on:

  • Building alliances across sectors
  • Connecting academia, industry, and government
  • Facilitating interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Creating sustainable AI ethics ecosystems

5. Public Engagement and Awareness

Projects aimed at:

  • Improving public understanding of AI
  • Encouraging informed discussions about AI ethics
  • Increasing public participation in AI-related decision-making
  • Transforming public discourse around emerging technologies

What Makes a Strong Application?

The Institute has clearly stated that it is looking for projects that are already on a well-defined path toward impact.

Applicants should note that this fellowship is not intended for:

  • Early-stage concepts
  • Idea-stage proposals
  • Proof-of-concept projects
  • Blue-sky research without implementation plans

Instead, successful applicants are expected to demonstrate:

Existing Project Development

Projects should already be underway, with evidence of meaningful progress.

Established Partnerships

Applicants should have:

  • Existing collaborators
  • Strategic partners already engaged
  • Clearly identified stakeholders who will support implementation

Defined Delivery Roadmap

Applications should include:

  • Concrete milestones
  • Realistic timelines
  • Deliverables
  • Implementation strategies

Impact Measurement Framework

Applicants must explain:

  • How impact will be achieved
  • How outcomes will be measured
  • What indicators will be used to evaluate success

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence is increasingly shaping economies, governments, healthcare systems, education, security, and everyday life.

As AI capabilities continue to expand, ethical considerations have become more important than ever. Questions around fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, safety, and societal impact require practical solutions that go beyond academic discussion.

The Accelerator Fellowship Programme seeks to bridge this gap by supporting leaders who can transform ethical AI research into actionable solutions that benefit society.

Fellowship Benefits

Although detailed funding information is provided within the fellowship’s expanded sections, participants can generally expect:

  • Affiliation with the University of Oxford’s Institute for Ethics in AI
  • Access to world-class expertise and networks
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Support in accelerating project impact
  • Increased visibility for ethical AI initiatives
  • Engagement with leading researchers, policymakers, and industry practitioners

Number of Fellowships Available

The Institute for Ethics in AI is seeking to recruit:

3–4 Accelerator Fellows

for the current cohort.

Given the limited number of positions available, applicants are encouraged to prepare strong, evidence-based proposals demonstrating both readiness and potential impact.

Application Process

Interested applicants should:

  1. Review the official eligibility criteria carefully.
  2. Ensure their project aligns with at least one Institute research priority.
  3. Demonstrate existing project development and partnerships.
  4. Prepare a clear impact strategy and implementation roadmap.
  5. Submit their application through the official Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI channels.

Apply Here 

Important Contact Information

For fellowship-related enquiries:

Email: aiethicsafp@philosophy.ox.ac.uk

For general enquiries:

Email: aiethicscomms@philosophy.ox.ac.uk

Final Thoughts

The Oxford Accelerator Fellowship Programme represents a unique opportunity for researchers, innovators, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and practitioners working at the intersection of artificial intelligence and ethics.

By supporting projects that are already positioned for impact, the programme aims to accelerate real-world solutions that promote responsible AI development, strengthen governance frameworks, build strategic collaborations, and improve public understanding of AI.

For individuals and teams committed to ensuring that artificial intelligence develops in ways that are ethical, inclusive, and socially beneficial, this fellowship offers access to one of the world’s most influential platforms for AI ethics leadership.

For more information on this fellowship visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

 

 

The post Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Scholars Incubator Program offered by the Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) is a prestigious international research development initiative designed to support, mentor, and empower emerging scholars in the fields of social sciences and MENA-focused research. This program is not just a short-term training course—it is a structured academic incubation ecosystem that provides junior […]

The post Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Scholars Incubator Program offered by the Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) is a prestigious international research development initiative designed to support, mentor, and empower emerging scholars in the fields of social sciences and MENA-focused research.

This program is not just a short-term training course—it is a structured academic incubation ecosystem that provides junior researchers, graduate students, and early-career scholars with the institutional support, mentorship, and research environment needed to advance their academic careers on a global scale.

Based in Europe and focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, SSL MENA is an independent, non-governmental, and non-partisan research institution that fosters interdisciplinary collaboration and academic excellence. The Scholars Incubator Program is one of its flagship initiatives, designed to bridge the gap between early academic potential and global research opportunities.

About Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA)

The Social Sciences Lab MENA is a European-based independent research institution dedicated to building a global scholarly community focused on MENA studies.

The Lab brings together researchers from different academic disciplines, ideologies, and regions to encourage collaboration, knowledge exchange, and high-quality research output.

Its core mission includes:

  • Developing interdisciplinary research networks focused on the MENA region
  • Supporting emerging scholars in academic publishing and funding applications
  • Providing institutional affiliation to researchers worldwide
  • Facilitating field research and research support services
  • Connecting scholars across senior and junior academic levels
  • Promoting independent research and intellectual freedom

Unlike traditional institutions, SSL MENA does not impose a fixed research agenda. Instead, it empowers scholars to pursue their own research interests with institutional guidance and support.

What is the Scholars Incubator Program?

The Scholars Incubator Program is a structured academic development fellowship designed to help emerging researchers strengthen their academic identity, research capabilities, and publication potential.

It provides participants with:

  • Academic mentorship and supervision
  • Research collaboration opportunities
  • Institutional affiliation with a European research institute
  • Publishing and funding application support
  • Access to academic networks and research communities

The program is designed to be both intellectually rigorous and professionally supportive, ensuring scholars develop practical research skills while advancing their academic careers.

Program Vision and Philosophy

The Scholars Incubator Program is built on the belief that researchers should have intellectual freedom, structured support, and collaborative opportunities.

Key principles include:

1. Researcher-Centered Approach

Participants are encouraged to develop independent research ideas rather than follow a fixed institutional agenda.

2. Inclusive Academic Environment

The program welcomes scholars from diverse backgrounds, ideologies, and academic traditions, provided they adhere to research ethics and academic standards.

3. Interdisciplinary Collaboration

Scholars from different disciplines work together to enrich research perspectives and outcomes.

4. Long-Term Academic Growth

The program is designed not just for short-term training but for long-term academic career development.

Key Benefits of the Scholars Incubator Program

Participants in the program gain access to a wide range of academic, professional, and institutional benefits.

1. Research Affiliation

  • One-year affiliation with the Social Sciences Lab MENA
  • Possible extension through continued engagement with the Lab Community
  • Recognition of academic participation and training

2. Mentorship and Academic Supervision

  • Personalized mentorship from experienced scholars
  • Guidance on research design and methodology
  • Support in developing academic career pathways

3. Publishing and Academic Writing Support

Participants receive:

  • Guidance on academic publishing processes
  • Editing and proofreading support
  • Assistance in preparing journal submissions
  • Feedback on research manuscripts

4. Funding and Career Development Support

The program provides structured guidance for:

  • Applying to research grants and fellowships
  • European funding schemes such as:
    • ERC (European Research Council)
    • Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions
    • Erasmus+ programs
  • Research position applications across academic institutions

5. Access to Research Resources

Scholars benefit from:

  • Access to research data and institutional resources
  • Participation in collaborative research projects
  • Fieldwork and methodological support
  • Integration into thematic research groups

6. Networking and Academic Community

Participants join a global scholarly network that includes:

  • Junior and senior researchers
  • Principal investigators
  • Guest scholars and experts
  • Interdisciplinary academic communities

This network enables long-term academic collaboration and professional development.

7. Certification and Institutional Recognition

Participants receive:

  • Affiliation certificate from SSL MENA
  • Recognition of training participation
  • Support letters when required for academic applications

Program Structure and Learning Experience

The Scholars Incubator Program is structured to provide a balanced academic and practical experience.

Core Activities Include:

1. Mentorship Sessions

  • 30 minutes monthly (individual sessions)
  • Focused academic guidance and progress review

2. Monthly Group Sessions

  • 1-hour collaborative academic discussions
  • Peer learning and research exchange

3. Workshops and Opportunities Sessions

  • 1-hour monthly training sessions
  • Focus on research tools, publishing, and funding applications

4. Focus Zone Sessions

  • 2-hour intensive group research discussions
  • Project development and methodological support

5. Research Groups (Bonus Component)

  • Participation based on availability of senior scholars and principal investigators
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary research projects

Research Environment and Academic Philosophy

SSL MENA emphasizes flexibility and intellectual independence while maintaining academic rigor.

Key features include:

  • No fixed daily schedule for activities
  • Flexible online participation model
  • Weekend-focused sessions to accommodate global participants
  • Clear deadlines for specific tasks
  • Strong emphasis on peer collaboration and academic excellence

Post-Program Opportunities and Long-Term Support

One of the strongest aspects of the program is its continued support after completion.

Participants gain access to the Lab Community, which provides:

  • Continued research collaboration opportunities
  • Access to funding calls and grant databases
  • Integration into research projects and teams
  • Ongoing mentorship and academic support
  • Opportunities to publish and present research
  • Career development across global academic networks

This ensures that scholars continue to grow beyond the formal program duration.

Eligibility Criteria

To participate in the Scholars Incubator Program, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree (BA or equivalent)
  • Graduated students or early-career scholars preferred

Language Requirements

  • Proficiency in Arabic is required
  • English proficiency is considered an advantage

Technical Requirements

  • Reliable internet access for online participation
  • Ability to engage in virtual meetings and collaborative sessions

Professional Requirements

  • Strong interest in academic research
  • Commitment to participating in structured activities
  • Willingness to collaborate with peers and senior scholars

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to nationals of MENA countries
  • Also open to international researchers with interest in MENA studies

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete the following steps:

Step 1: Application Submission

  • Complete the official application form via the program portal

Step 2: Program Fee

  • Pay a €100 program participation fee

Important Note

  • Admission is based on a first-come, first-served basis
  • Early application is strongly recommended due to limited spots

Program Duration and Timeline

  • One-year research affiliation
  • Program activities are ongoing with structured milestones
  • Possible extension through Lab Community engagement

 Why You Should Apply

The Scholars Incubator Program offers a rare opportunity for emerging scholars to gain institutional affiliation, mentorship, and research development support within a globally connected academic environment.

Unlike traditional academic programs, it prioritizes independent research, interdisciplinary collaboration, and long-term academic growth.

For researchers seeking to publish, secure funding, and build a strong academic career in MENA studies or social sciences more broadly, this program provides a powerful platform for development and international exposure.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Helsinki RESDOC 2026: Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Research Programme in Finland with €2,900 Monthly Salary, International Mobility, MSCA COFUND Funding, and World-Class Research Training
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The University of Helsinki has launched applications for the prestigious RESDOC Doctoral Training Programme 2026, a fully funded international doctoral research initiative designed to train the next generation of researchers capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing sustainability, health, environmental, and societal challenges. Co-funded by the European Union through the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions […]

The post University of Helsinki RESDOC 2026: Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Research Programme in Finland with €2,900 Monthly Salary, International Mobility, MSCA COFUND Funding, and World-Class Research Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Helsinki has launched applications for the prestigious RESDOC Doctoral Training Programme 2026, a fully funded international doctoral research initiative designed to train the next generation of researchers capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing sustainability, health, environmental, and societal challenges.

Co-funded by the European Union through the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) COFUND Programme under Horizon Europe and the University of Helsinki, RESDOC offers selected doctoral researchers a unique opportunity to pursue a four-year fully funded doctoral degree in Finland while receiving structured research training, international collaboration opportunities, interdisciplinary supervision, and competitive employment conditions.

Applications are open from 1 June 2026 to 31 August 2026.

Application Deadline

31 August 2026 (11:59 PM Finnish Time – EET)

What is the RESDOC Programme?

RESDOC (Training Doctoral Excellence for a Resilient and Just Future) is an innovative doctoral training programme established at the University of Helsinki to prepare researchers capable of generating solutions for complex global challenges.

The programme is anchored within the University’s major research profiling initiative, Resilient and Just Systems (RESET), and combines:

  • High-quality doctoral research
  • International collaboration
  • Interdisciplinary training
  • Industry engagement
  • Public-sector partnerships
  • Career development support
  • Research mobility opportunities

Successful candidates will become salaried doctoral researchers employed by the University of Helsinki while pursuing their doctoral degree.

Why Choose RESDOC?

RESDOC goes beyond traditional doctoral education by combining scientific excellence with transferable skills training and real-world engagement.

Participants will benefit from:

  • Full-time employment for four years
  • Monthly gross salary of approximately €2,900
  • World-class supervision
  • International research exposure
  • Industry and public-sector collaborations
  • Career development training
  • Interdisciplinary research opportunities
  • Access to one of Europe’s leading research universities
  • Structured doctoral training aligned with European standards

Research Focus and Environment

One of the most distinctive features of RESDOC is its interdisciplinary approach.

Doctoral researchers are free to develop their own research topics provided they align with one of the programme’s four major thematic areas.

The Four RESDOC Research Themes

1. Infectious Diseases and Antimicrobial Resistance in Changing Environments

Research under this theme focuses on:

  • Emerging infectious diseases
  • Public health threats
  • Antimicrobial resistance
  • Environmental drivers of disease
  • Global health resilience
  • Disease surveillance systems

2. Biodiversity and Nature’s Resilience

Researchers may explore:

  • Ecosystem sustainability
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Climate adaptation
  • Ecological resilience
  • Environmental restoration
  • Nature-based solutions

3. Secure and Sustainable Food and Water Systems

Potential topics include:

  • Food security
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Water governance
  • Resource management
  • Food systems transformation
  • Agricultural resilience

4. Just Socio-Ecological Transition

This theme addresses:

  • Climate justice
  • Social sustainability
  • Environmental governance
  • Green transitions
  • Policy innovation
  • Equitable development

Outstanding Research Environment

Doctoral researchers will conduct their studies within one of the University of Helsinki’s 14 doctoral programmes while simultaneously participating in the interdisciplinary RESDOC community.

This dual structure provides:

  • Strong disciplinary expertise
  • Cross-disciplinary collaboration
  • Access to international networks
  • Advanced research infrastructure
  • Academic mentoring
  • Professional development opportunities

Supervision and Academic Support

Every RESDOC doctoral researcher receives extensive academic support throughout the programme.

Each candidate is assigned:

Primary Supervisor

A principal investigator (PI) from the University of Helsinki responsible for guiding the doctoral research.

Co-Supervisor

An additional supervisor who may come from:

  • The University of Helsinki
  • Industry partners
  • Public sector organizations
  • International institutions

Thesis Committee

Every doctoral researcher receives support from a Thesis Committee that monitors research progress and provides guidance.

Peer Mentoring Groups

Researchers participate in peer support groups that encourage collaboration, networking, and community building.

Doctoral Studies and Training

The doctoral degree combines original research with structured coursework.

Degree Structure

The programme consists of:

  • Doctoral dissertation
  • Mandatory studies totaling 30 ECTS credits

RESDOC contributes approximately:

  • 20–25 ECTS credits through specialized training modules

Additional credits are earned through discipline-specific doctoral programme courses.

RESDOC Training Components

Doctoral Base Camp

During the first year, participants attend an immersive retreat featuring:

  • Expert workshops
  • Research skills training
  • Interdisciplinary discussions
  • Networking opportunities
  • Community building activities

Annual Symposia

Every year researchers participate in symposia involving:

  • Supervisors
  • Industry partners
  • Associated organizations
  • International experts

Researchers’ Day

Doctoral researchers organize annual thematic events focused on:

  • Systems thinking
  • Sustainability challenges
  • Scientific collaboration
  • Cross-disciplinary learning

Artificial Intelligence Training

Participants complete mandatory AI training covering:

  • Responsible AI use
  • Research applications
  • Critical assessment of AI tools

Innovation and Entrepreneurship Module

Training includes:

  • Innovation development
  • Entrepreneurial thinking
  • Commercialization skills
  • Industry engagement

Citizen Science Assignments

Researchers engage communities through:

  • Public participation projects
  • Science communication
  • Citizen engagement activities

Compulsory International and Intersectoral Secondments

A defining feature of RESDOC is its compulsory secondment programme.

Each doctoral researcher completes a:

  • Two- to four-month secondment

These placements may occur in:

  • Industry
  • Government organizations
  • Research institutions
  • International organizations

Benefits include:

  • Practical experience
  • Career networking
  • Knowledge transfer
  • Exposure beyond academia

Importantly, doctoral researchers continue receiving their salary during secondments.

Extensive International Partnership Network

RESDOC collaborates with 23 associated partner organizations across academia, government, and industry.

Partners include:

Private Sector

  • Orion
  • UPM Kymmene Oyj
  • Valio
  • Raisio Group
  • Resistomap
  • Vetcare
  • Demos Helsinki

Public Sector

  • City of Helsinki
  • City of Lahti
  • Finnish Environment Institute (SYKE)
  • VTT
  • HUS Diagnostics
  • Natural Resources Institute Finland

International Universities

  • University of Nairobi
  • University College Dublin
  • University of Göttingen
  • University of Turku
  • Linnaeus University
  • Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences

Employment and Working Conditions

Successful applicants become employees of the University of Helsinki.

Employment Duration

  • Four years
  • 2027–2030

Salary

Doctoral researchers receive:

  • Approximately €2,900 gross per month

Additional Benefits

Employees enjoy:

  • Occupational healthcare
  • Social security coverage
  • Paid annual leave
  • Flexible working arrangements
  • Professional development support
  • Safe and inclusive working environment

Living in Finland

Selected researchers will relocate to Finland and reside there throughout the programme.

Benefits of living in Finland include:

  • High quality of life
  • Excellent public services
  • Safe society
  • Strong research ecosystem
  • International academic environment
  • Nature-rich surroundings
  • English-friendly environment

Equality, Diversity and Inclusion

RESDOC follows the principles of the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions and strongly promotes:

  • Equal opportunities
  • Diversity
  • Inclusion
  • Accessibility
  • Non-discrimination

The programme also supports researchers at risk whose academic freedom or career opportunities may be limited in their home countries.

RESDOC Framework and Funding

RESDOC is funded through one of Europe’s most prestigious research funding mechanisms.

Funding Source

The programme is co-funded by:

  • The European Union Horizon Europe Programme
  • Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) COFUND
  • University of Helsinki

Why MSCA Funding Matters

MSCA is widely recognized as the European Union’s flagship programme for researcher development.

Since 1996, MSCA has supported more than 150,000 researchers worldwide through:

  • International mobility
  • Research excellence
  • Career development
  • Cross-sector collaboration

RESDOC operates under Grant Agreement No. 101261796.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the University of Helsinki’s online recruitment system.

Step 1: Verify Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Hold a Master’s degree
  • Not hold a PhD
  • Have less than four years of research experience
  • Meet English language requirements
  • Meet mobility requirements
  • Be willing to live in Finland

Step 2: Prepare Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Europass CV
  2. Motivation Letter and Doctoral Study Plan
  3. Degree Certificate and Transcript
  4. Proof of English Language Proficiency
  5. Research Proposal, Ethics Self-Assessment and Mobility Plan

Step 3: Submit Application

Applications must be submitted no later than:

31 August 2026 at 11:59 PM EET

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Selection Process Timeline

June–August 2026

Application period

September 2026

Eligibility screening and evaluation

October 2026

Academic alignment interviews

November 2026

Final selection and supervisor matching

December 2026

Employment preparation and relocation guidance

January–February 2027

Programme commencement

Final Thoughts

The University of Helsinki RESDOC Doctoral Programme represents one of the most attractive fully funded doctoral opportunities currently available in Europe. Combining a competitive salary, international research training, interdisciplinary collaboration, world-class supervision, and extensive industry engagement, RESDOC offers exceptional preparation for future careers in academia, government, international organizations, and industry.

Researchers passionate about sustainability, health, biodiversity, food systems, climate action, and social transformation are strongly encouraged to apply before the 31 August 2026 deadline.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post University of Helsinki RESDOC 2026: Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Research Programme in Finland with €2,900 Monthly Salary, International Mobility, MSCA COFUND Funding, and World-Class Research Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid United Nations Internships for Youth 2026: IOM, UNDP, and UNOPS Open International Opportunities in Türkiye, The Gambia, Malaysia, and Thailand
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
For young professionals and students dreaming of launching a career within the United Nations system, 2026 presents an exciting opportunity. Several UN agencies have opened fully paid internship opportunities across different regions of the world, offering participants valuable international exposure, professional development, and hands-on experience in global development, migration management, sustainable development, communications, administration, monitoring […]

The post Paid United Nations Internships for Youth 2026: IOM, UNDP, and UNOPS Open International Opportunities in Türkiye, The Gambia, Malaysia, and Thailand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
For young professionals and students dreaming of launching a career within the United Nations system, 2026 presents an exciting opportunity. Several UN agencies have opened fully paid internship opportunities across different regions of the world, offering participants valuable international exposure, professional development, and hands-on experience in global development, migration management, sustainable development, communications, administration, monitoring and evaluation, and supply chain operations.

Among the organizations currently recruiting interns are the International Organization for Migration (IOM), the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), and the United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS). These prestigious institutions are seeking talented, motivated, and passionate young individuals to contribute to their missions while gaining practical experience in multicultural and international work environments.

These internships provide monthly stipends and the opportunity to work directly with experienced professionals while supporting initiatives aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Why Consider a United Nations Internship?

Interning with a UN agency can be a transformative career experience. Participants gain:

  • Practical international work experience
  • Exposure to global development and humanitarian operations
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Enhanced understanding of UN systems and procedures
  • Mentorship from experienced international professionals
  • Opportunities to develop technical and leadership skills
  • Valuable experience that strengthens future job and graduate school applications
  • Financial support through internship stipends

The current opportunities span multiple sectors and allow candidates to work on meaningful projects that positively impact communities worldwide.

 

1. IOM Supply Chain Intern – Ankara, Türkiye

Application Deadline: 9 June 2026

The International Organization for Migration is recruiting a Supply Chain Intern in Ankara, Türkiye. This internship offers a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates interested in logistics, procurement, supply chain management, business administration, and humanitarian operations.

Internship Duration

  • Initial duration: 3 months
  • Internship category: Paid internship
  • Contract type: Internship (2–6 months)

About IOM

Established in 1951, IOM serves as the leading United Nations agency dedicated to migration. The organization works closely with governments, civil society organizations, and international partners to promote safe, orderly, and humane migration worldwide.

Key Responsibilities

The selected intern will support logistics and procurement operations by:

  • Following procurement and logistics documentation processes
  • Managing filing and archiving systems
  • Maintaining procurement records and contracts
  • Preparing procurement documentation
  • Supporting goods receipt verification procedures
  • Assisting with payment processing and financial documentation
  • Coordinating with finance teams regarding vendor payments
  • Supporting other logistics and supply chain activities

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in the final year of a bachelor’s degree or have graduated within the last 12 months
  • Study Social Sciences, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, or related disciplines
  • Be enrolled in a graduate degree programme, or possess equivalent qualifications
  • Be between 20 and 36 years old
  • Have less than two years of relevant work experience

Language Requirements

Candidates must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English
  • Fluency in Turkish

Benefits

  • Monthly internship stipend
  • Professional experience within a UN agency
  • Occupational accident and illness coverage
  • Exposure to international procurement and logistics operations
  • Opportunity to work with migration-related programmes

 

2. IOM Monitoring and Evaluation Intern – Banjul, The Gambia

Application Deadline: 10 June 2026

The International Organization for Migration is also seeking a Monitoring and Evaluation Intern in Banjul, The Gambia.

This internship supports the implementation of the Migrant Protection Return and Reintegration for Sub-Saharan Africa (MPRR-SSA) initiative and provides practical experience in programme monitoring, migration governance, data management, and impact assessment.

Internship Duration

  • Initial duration: 6 months
  • Paid internship

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will:

  • Support monitoring activities related to reintegration programmes
  • Assist with beneficiary tracking and database management
  • Participate in field monitoring visits
  • Assess reintegration support outcomes
  • Contribute to the development of monitoring tools
  • Support monthly monitoring reports
  • Conduct data cleaning and analysis
  • Assist with impact assessments of grants and community projects
  • Monitor gender, age, and vulnerability indicators

Academic Requirements

Applicants should be:

  • Final-year university students or
  • Enrolled in advanced tertiary education programmes or
  • Recent graduates

Preferred fields include:

  • Development Studies
  • Social Sciences
  • Law
  • Humanitarian Affairs
  • Related disciplines

Required Skills

Candidates should possess:

  • Strong analytical skills
  • Excellent writing abilities
  • Good communication skills
  • Knowledge of Microsoft Office tools
  • Familiarity with Kobo Collect and Visio (advantage)

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English
  • Fluency in at least one local language

Benefits

  • Monthly stipend
  • Hands-on monitoring and evaluation experience
  • Field exposure to migration and reintegration programmes
  • Experience working with development and humanitarian data
  • Professional networking opportunities

 

3. UNDP Communications and Programmes Internship – Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

Application Deadline: 16 June 2026

The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is offering a Communications and Programmes Internship within the Malaysia-UN SDG Trust Fund Secretariat.

The internship provides an exceptional opportunity for candidates interested in communications, public relations, media, social impact, sustainable development, and programme management.

Internship Duration

  • 3 months
  • Full-time internship

About the Malaysia-UN SDG Trust Fund

The Fund supports initiatives that advance the Sustainable Development Goals in Malaysia, focusing particularly on vulnerable populations and communities at risk of being left behind.

The Fund promotes collaboration among government agencies, civil society organizations, NGOs, and development partners to achieve inclusive and sustainable development.

Key Responsibilities

The intern will:

  • Create social media content
  • Support communication campaigns
  • Draft captions and promotional materials
  • Develop simple graphics and visual content
  • Monitor stakeholder and grantee social media platforms
  • Assist with event organization and documentation
  • Conduct research and media due diligence
  • Support grant review processes
  • Assist with monitoring and evaluation activities

Academic Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be in the final year of a bachelor’s degree; or
  • Have graduated within the previous year; or
  • Be participating in a placement or traineeship programme

Preferred academic backgrounds include:

  • Mass Communication
  • Journalism
  • Public Relations
  • Marketing
  • Graphic Design
  • Media Studies

Language Requirements

Candidates must possess working proficiency in:

  • English
  • Bahasa Malaysia

Technical Skills

Preferred skills include:

  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Microsoft Teams
  • Canva
  • Adobe Creative Suite
  • CapCut
  • Video editing tools

Benefits

  • Monthly stipend of RM637.50
  • Experience supporting SDG implementation
  • Exposure to UN communications and programme management
  • Opportunities to engage with development partners and communities
  • Enhanced professional portfolio development

 

4. UNOPS Administration Intern – Bangkok, Thailand

Application Deadline: 7 June 2026

UNOPS is recruiting an Administration Intern based in Bangkok, Thailand.

The position supports the Southeast Asia Energy Transition Partnership (ETP), a programme promoting sustainable and renewable energy development across Southeast Asia.

Internship Duration

  • Six months
  • Possibility of extension up to one year

About the Programme

The Southeast Asia Energy Transition Partnership works closely with governments and stakeholders to support cleaner energy systems, climate action, and sustainable development.

The programme primarily focuses on:

  • Indonesia
  • Philippines
  • Vietnam

Key Responsibilities

The selected intern will:

  • Organize meetings and events
  • Prepare meeting documentation
  • Coordinate travel arrangements
  • Support procurement and contract management
  • Process project transactions
  • Assist with Steering Committee meetings
  • Compile programme reports
  • Maintain databases and records
  • Support administrative and fund management functions

Academic Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree or equivalent
  • Be enrolled in a bachelor’s, master’s, or PhD programme; or
  • Have graduated within the previous three years

Preferred fields include:

  • Public Administration
  • Development Studies
  • Social Sciences
  • Related disciplines

Language Requirements

  • Fluent English required

Preferred Experience

  • Administration
  • International Development
  • Project Support
  • Procurement
  • Contract Management

Benefits

  • Paid internship
  • Potential extension to one year
  • Exposure to regional sustainable energy initiatives
  • International work experience
  • Professional development within UNOPS

 

5. IOM Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) Intern – Athens, Greece

Application Deadline: Check Official Vacancy Announcement

The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is also offering an exciting internship opportunity for aspiring professionals interested in mental health, psychosocial support, migration, humanitarian assistance, and community well-being. Based in Athens, Greece, the Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) Intern position provides an excellent opportunity to gain practical experience within one of the world’s leading migration organizations.

As migration challenges continue to affect millions of people worldwide, mental health and psychosocial support services have become increasingly important components of humanitarian and development responses. Through this internship, successful candidates will contribute to initiatives aimed at improving the psychological well-being and social resilience of migrants, refugees, and vulnerable populations.

About the Internship

The intern will support the implementation of mental health and psychosocial support programmes and activities under the supervision of experienced professionals. The position offers valuable exposure to humanitarian programming, case management systems, psychosocial interventions, and community-based support mechanisms.

Working within a multicultural and international environment, interns will gain firsthand knowledge of how mental health services are integrated into migration and humanitarian programmes.

Key Responsibilities

Depending on programme needs, the selected intern may support:

  • Mental health and psychosocial support activities for migrants and refugees.
  • Community outreach and awareness initiatives.
  • Documentation and reporting of programme activities.
  • Data collection, analysis, and monitoring.
  • Preparation of training materials and awareness campaigns.
  • Coordination with implementing partners and stakeholders.
  • Research on mental health and psychosocial support best practices.
  • Administrative and logistical support for programme implementation.

Learning Opportunities

The internship provides opportunities to:

  • Develop practical experience in humanitarian mental health programming.
  • Learn about international migration and protection frameworks.
  • Strengthen project management and coordination skills.
  • Enhance monitoring, reporting, and data management competencies.
  • Gain exposure to United Nations operational procedures.
  • Build professional networks within the humanitarian and development sectors.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants are generally expected to:

  • Be enrolled in a university programme or have recently graduated.
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in mental health, psychology, social work, public health, humanitarian affairs, migration studies, or related disciplines.
  • Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Demonstrate commitment to diversity, inclusion, and humanitarian principles.
  • Meet IOM internship eligibility requirements regarding age and academic status.

Benefits

Participants may receive:

  • Monthly internship stipend (subject to IOM internship policies).
  • Professional mentoring and supervision.
  • International humanitarian work experience.
  • Exposure to migration and protection programmes.
  • Networking opportunities with UN and international development professionals.
  • Valuable experience that can support future careers within the UN system and international organizations.

 

Why These UN Internships Stand Out

These opportunities represent some of the most attractive entry points into the United Nations system. Unlike many internships globally, these positions provide financial support through stipends while offering meaningful responsibilities and direct involvement in real-world development projects.

Participants gain exposure to:

  • International migration management
  • Monitoring and evaluation systems
  • Sustainable Development Goals implementation
  • Strategic communications
  • Programme management
  • Administrative operations
  • Supply chain management
  • Climate and energy transition initiatives

For students and recent graduates seeking careers within international organizations, these internships offer an excellent foundation for future employment opportunities in the development and humanitarian sectors.

Important Application Tips

  1. Apply as early as possible before the deadline.
  2. Tailor your CV and cover letter to each specific internship.
  3. Highlight volunteer experience, leadership roles, and relevant coursework.
  4. Demonstrate your commitment to international development and UN values.
  5. Showcase language skills and digital competencies.
  6. Carefully review all eligibility criteria before applying.
  7. Prepare supporting documents in advance.

With application deadlines approaching quickly, interested candidates should begin preparing their applications immediately and submit them through the respective official recruitment platforms.

 

More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Paid United Nations Internships for Youth 2026: IOM, UNDP, and UNOPS Open International Opportunities in Türkiye, The Gambia, Malaysia, and Thailand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Futures Mobility Fund 2026: Comprehensive Guide for Applicants in Australia
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The New Futures Mobility Fund, delivered by the Australian Academy of Science, is a major national initiative designed to empower humanitarian visa holders living in Australia by supporting their participation in recognised scientific conferences, symposia, workshops, and other STEM‑focused professional events across the country. This program removes financial barriers, strengthens research visibility, and helps emerging […]

The post New Futures Mobility Fund 2026: Comprehensive Guide for Applicants in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New Futures Mobility Fund, delivered by the Australian Academy of Science, is a major national initiative designed to empower humanitarian visa holders living in Australia by supporting their participation in recognised scientific conferences, symposia, workshops, and other STEM‑focused professional events across the country. This program removes financial barriers, strengthens research visibility, and helps emerging and established researchers build meaningful professional networks within Australia’s scientific community.

Overview of the New Futures Mobility Fund

The New Futures Mobility Fund provides competitive mobility grants to eligible applicants who wish to attend structured scientific or professional development events in Australia. These grants are intended to:

  • Strengthen research capability
  • Expand professional networks
  • Enhance career development
  • Increase visibility within Australia’s STEM community
  • Remove financial barriers that prevent participation in scientific events

The program is made possible through a generous gift from Jaishree Srinivasan and Academy Fellow Emeritus Professor Srini Srinivasan AM FAA FRS.

Key Dates for the 2026 Application Cycle

Applicants must take note of the following timeline:

  • 18 May 2026 – Applications open
  • 09 July 2026 – Applications close (deadline)
  • September 2026 – Notification of outcome
  • October 2026 – Funding agreements issued and funded activities may commence

Program Highlights

The New Futures Mobility Fund offers a range of benefits designed to support professional growth:

  1. Funding support to attend recognised scientific conferences and similar STEM events in Australia
  2. Career development opportunities through networking and visibility
  3. Flexible funding to reduce cost barriers and increase accessibility
  4. Support for research training, research activity, and professional development
  5. Opportunities to engage with Australia’s scientific community and expand research networks

1. Eligibility Requirements

Applicant Eligibility

To apply, individuals must meet all the following criteria at the time of application:

  • Be living in Australia
  • Hold an eligible humanitarian visa subclass
  • Be engaged in research training or research activity in Australia
  • Be seeking funding to attend a recognised STEM scientific conference, symposium, workshop, or similar event within Australia

Eligible Humanitarian Visa Subclasses

  • Refugee (subclass 200)
  • In-country Special Humanitarian (subclass 201)
  • Global Special Humanitarian visa (subclass 202)**
  • Emergency Rescue (subclass 203)
  • Woman at Risk (subclass 204)
  • Protection visa (subclass 866)**

Note: As of 29 May 2026, subclasses 866 and 202 have been newly added as eligible visa types.

Eligible Applicant Profiles

  • Masters students
  • PhD candidates
  • Early- and mid-career researchers (EMCRs) with a Masters or PhD awarded up to 15 years prior to the application deadline
    • Extensions may be considered for significant career interruptions

2. Eligible Activities

Activities must be Australia-based and must clearly demonstrate how participation will strengthen the applicant’s research capability, networks, and career development.

Eligible Activities Include:

  • Recognised scientific conferences, symposia, and workshops
  • Specialist training courses or masterclasses linked to scientific events
  • Professional development opportunities supporting research training or activity
  • Research translation or industry engagement forums with a STEM focus
  • Other professional opportunities approved by the Academy

The Academy may request clarification if the scientific relevance of an event is unclear.

3. Fields of Research

Applications are welcome from all STEM fields, including:

  • Science
  • Technology
  • Engineering
  • Mathematics

4. Eligible Australian Research Organisations (EROs)

Applicants must be employed by or affiliated with an eligible research organisation, such as:

  • Australian tertiary education institutions
  • Cooperative research centres
  • Publicly funded research agencies
  • State or territory-funded research organisations
  • Public or private Australian research companies
  • Australian not-for-profit research organisations
  • Other incorporated Australian entities

Note:

  • EROs must be registered for GST
  • Universities must be income tax exempt
  • Non-corporate Commonwealth entities are not eligible

5. Additional Program Information

Terms of Award

Covers funding conditions, allowable expenses, and grant management requirements.

Application Process

Applicants must submit through the Academy’s online portal and provide all required documentation.

Assessment Process

Applications are evaluated based on eligibility, merit, relevance of the event, and potential impact on the applicant’s research career.

Reporting Requirements

Successful applicants must submit a final report detailing outcomes and benefits gained from the funded activity.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant opportunities here

 

The post New Futures Mobility Fund 2026: Comprehensive Guide for Applicants in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GIZ Trainee Programmes: Launch Your Global Development Career with World-Class Training
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH offers some of the most respected and competitive trainee programmes in the international development sector. Designed for young professionals seeking hands-on experience, global exposure, and structured professional growth, these programmes prepare future leaders for impactful careers in development cooperation, financial management, and strategic communications. GIZ’s trainee tracks […]

The post GIZ Trainee Programmes: Launch Your Global Development Career with World-Class Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH offers some of the most respected and competitive trainee programmes in the international development sector. Designed for young professionals seeking hands-on experience, global exposure, and structured professional growth, these programmes prepare future leaders for impactful careers in development cooperation, financial management, and strategic communications.

GIZ’s trainee tracks include:

  • German Development Cooperation (DC) Trainee Programme
  • Financial Management Trainee Programme (International Locations)
  • Public Relations (PR) Traineeship

Each programme provides a unique pathway into the world of international cooperation, offering structured training, mentorship, and opportunities to work across Germany and GIZ partner countries worldwide.

1. German Development Cooperation (DC) Trainee Programme

The DC Trainee Programme is implemented by GIZ on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ). It is specifically designed to cultivate the next generation of development cooperation professionals.

What the DC Trainee Programme Offers

Participants gain deep insight into:

  • Coordination and management processes in development cooperation
  • Policy frameworks and implementation strategies
  • Project cycle management
  • International development structures and partnerships
  • Technical and managerial responsibilities within development organizations

Upon completion, trainees are equipped to take on demanding roles in German and international development institutions.

Key Features

  • Structured rotations across GIZ departments
  • Exposure to global development challenges
  • Professional mentorship
  • Preparation for technical and leadership roles

2. Financial Management Trainee Programme (International Locations)

This 18‑month programme is ideal for graduates in business administration or related fields who want to build a career in international financial management.

Programme Structure

The programme includes:

  1. Three-week onboarding in Germany
  2. Rotations (2–8 weeks each) across key GIZ units, including:
    • Finance
    • Procurement and Contracting
    • International Services
    • Regional Departments
    • Legal Affairs and Insurance
    • Internal Audit and Compliance
  3. A 12‑month international assignment, where trainees:
    • Work on a full business administration cycle
    • Manage financial processes in a GIZ project
    • Receive supervision and mentorship
  4. Eight weeks of job shadowing at a GIZ country office

What Trainees Gain

  • Practical experience in international financial management
  • Exposure to global project operations
  • Hands-on learning in real development contexts
  • Professional growth through structured training

3. Public Relations (PR) Traineeship

This 24‑month traineeship is designed for individuals passionate about communications and international cooperation. It prepares trainees for modern PR roles within a global organization.

What the PR Traineeship Includes

  • Training within GIZ’s Corporate Communications Unit
  • Writing press releases, online content, and strategic communication materials
  • Organizing internal and external events
  • Managing corporate image and brand consistency
  • Moderating social intranet communities
  • Advising communication teams worldwide

Certified Training and External Exposure
