  • Participation in accredited PR-sector seminars
  • Completion of a recognized industry qualification
  • Job shadowing at a media company
  • Support from GIZ’s press team in selecting a placement

Most training takes place in Bonn and Eschborn, with additional exposure to GIZ’s work in a partner country.

Benefits of GIZ Trainee Programmes

  • Flexible working hours
  • International exposure
  • Equal opportunities
  • Extensive training and continuing education
  • Mentorship and professional guidance
  • Strong career prospects within GIZ

Application Deadlines

GIZ updates application windows throughout the year. Applicants should regularly check the official GIZ career portal for current deadlines and openings.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit  here

Discover more  trainee opportunities here 

 

The post GIZ Trainee Programmes: Launch Your Global Development Career with World-Class Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations 2026 — Comprehensive Guide
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations stands as one of the most respected recognitions for emerging scholars whose work advances global understanding of soft power, international cultural relations, and Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy (FCEP). For over 25 years, the award has celebrated academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and research that strengthens cultural diplomacy […]

The post The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations 2026 — Comprehensive Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations stands as one of the most respected recognitions for emerging scholars whose work advances global understanding of soft power, international cultural relations, and Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy (FCEP). For over 25 years, the award has celebrated academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and research that strengthens cultural diplomacy worldwide.

This detailed article provides a full overview of the 2026 call for applications, eligibility requirements, submission guidelines, benefits, jury composition, and the award’s long-standing legacy.

ifa Research Award 2026: A Deep Dive into One of the Leading Global Awards in Cultural Relations

The Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen (ifa) has long been a global leader in promoting international cultural dialogue. Through its annual Research Award, ifa supports young scholars whose work contributes meaningfully to the understanding and practice of cultural diplomacy.

The award is designed not only to recognize academic excellence but also to amplify the visibility of emerging researchers, support publication of their work, and strengthen the global discourse on cultural relations.

Purpose and Vision of the Award

The award aims to:

  • Strengthen academic reflection on soft power, international cultural relations, and Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy.
  • Promote interdisciplinary exchange and broaden perspectives in cultural diplomacy.
  • Bring ifa’s international expertise into German and global debates.
  • Support young scholars at different career stages by offering flexible, tailored visibility formats.
  • Encourage research that has practical relevance for cultural policy, institutions, and intercultural practice.

Over the past 25 years, 27 scholars have been honored, each contributing to the global understanding of cultural diplomacy.

What the Award Includes

Award recipients receive a comprehensive support package designed to elevate their research and professional visibility:

1. Public Presentation of Research

Awardees can choose a format best suited to their work and career stage, such as:

  • Public lecture
  • Podcast
  • Short video
  • Other innovative formats

The goal is to ensure maximum visibility and accessibility of the research.

2. Publication Support

ifa covers the printing costs for publishing the award-winning work in its prestigious series:

  • “Kultur und Außenpolitik”
  • Published by Nomos Verlag
  • Available via open access
  • Subject to publisher review

This ensures global reach and academic credibility.

Eligibility Criteria

Applications must be submitted by a recommending professor on behalf of the young academic.

To qualify:

  1. Thesis Completion Timeline
    • Must be completed within the last two years
    • No earlier than 30 April 2024
  2. Academic Performance
    • Doctoral theses: at least magna cum laude
    • Master’s theses: at least the second-highest grade
  3. Research Focus The thesis must:
    • Examine soft power, international cultural relations, or Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy
    • Demonstrate the significance and effectiveness of these concepts
    • Advance theoretical or practical understanding
    • Offer applicability to cultural policy, institutions, or intercultural practice
    • Be an independent and sound scholarly achievement
  4. Recommendation Requirement
    • A formal recommendation from a professor is mandatory.

Required Application Documents

The recommending professor must submit one single PDF file (max. 15 MB) containing:

  • Complete text of the thesis (PDF)
  • Abstract
  • Letter of recommendation
  • Full assessment or grading documentation (German or English)
  • Declaration of independent work + date of completion/publication
  • Curriculum vitae
  • List of publications (if applicable)
  • Applicant’s contact information (email + institutional affiliation)

Language: Submissions may be in German or English.

Submission Deadline

30 June 2026 — 11:59 p.m. (CEST)

Selection Process and Jury

Award winners are chosen by ifa based on recommendations from an independent jury of experts.

Jury for 2026

  • Professor Nicole Colin (Aix-Marseille University)
  • Professor Eveline Dürr (LMU Munich)
  • Professor Julius Heinicke (University of Hildesheim)

Legacy of Excellence: Past Award Winners

The award has honored outstanding scholars since 2001, including:

  • 2021 – Hendrik W. Ohnesorge
  • 2020 – Tobias Reichard
  • 2019 – Dominik Herzner
  • 2018 – Elisabeth Piller & Sonja Großmann
  • 2017 – Lara Jüssen & Manuela Sato-Prinz
  • 2016 – Elena Korowin & Jens Adam
  • 2015 – Annika Hampel & Katayon Meier
  • …and many more distinguished researchers.

Additional special recognitions have also been awarded in various years.

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more  award opportunities here

The post The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations 2026 — Comprehensive Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Bangladesh Climate & Private Sector Reporting Fellowships 2026: A Complete Guide for Journalists
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN) has announced a major opportunity for journalists in Bangladesh: the 2026 Reporting Fellowships to Produce In‑Depth Stories on Climate Change and the Private Sector. This fellowship aims to strengthen high‑quality, deeply reported journalism that explores how industries, businesses, farmers, and workers in Bangladesh are both affected by—and contributing to—climate change. […]

The post Bangladesh Climate & Private Sector Reporting Fellowships 2026: A Complete Guide for Journalists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Earth Journalism Network (EJN) has announced a major opportunity for journalists in Bangladesh: the 2026 Reporting Fellowships to Produce In‑Depth Stories on Climate Change and the Private Sector. This fellowship aims to strengthen high‑quality, deeply reported journalism that explores how industries, businesses, farmers, and workers in Bangladesh are both affected by—and contributing to—climate change.

Application Deadline: June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM (Asia/Dhaka) Accepted Application Languages: English and Bangla

This article provides a full breakdown of the fellowship, including its purpose, themes, eligibility, budget requirements, judging criteria, and application process.

Overview: Why This Fellowship Matters

Bangladesh’s climate reporting has traditionally focused on rural communities and coastal vulnerabilities. However, climate change is also reshaping the private sector, from agriculture to garments to pharmaceuticals.

The uploaded document states:

“A 2025 World Bank study found that 25 million workdays were lost in 2024 due to heat‑related physical and mental health issues.” “Industries in Bangladesh cannot afford to ignore their two‑way relationships with climate change.”

Key issues include:

  • Heat stress reducing productivity in factories
  • Erratic weather damaging agricultural output
  • Environmental degradation caused by industries
  • Upcoming EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) impacting textile exports
  • Climate‑driven disruptions affecting farmers and fishers
  • Growing need for climate‑responsible business practices

EJN’s fellowship supports journalists who can illuminate these complex relationships and highlight solutions emerging across Bangladesh.

Fellowship Themes

Applicants may pitch stories related to:

  1. Impacts of climate change on Bangladesh’s private sector and how businesses are responding
  2. Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) rules and their influence
  3. Climate finance instruments, including carbon markets
  4. New business opportunities such as renewable energy and clean mobility
  5. Artificial intelligence and its climate implications
  6. Corporate greenwashing and accountability

Proposals with fresh, underreported angles are strongly preferred.

Eligibility Requirements

According to the uploaded document:

“Applicants can be from Bangladesh only.” “Applications are open to journalists working in any medium… We welcome applications from early‑career journalists and experienced reporters.”

Eligibility includes:

  • Must be based in Bangladesh
  • Open to individual journalists or groups (with one lead applicant)
  • Open to print, online, TV, radio, and other reporting professionals
  • Open to freelancers and staff journalists
  • Applications accepted in English or Bangla
  • Applicants must disclose any use of generative AI
  • Applicants must submit two samples of work
  • Applicants must include a letter of support from an editor committing to publish by September 30, 2026

Fellowship Logistics

  • Up to 10 fellowships will be awarded
  • Fellows will receive funding + mentorship
  • Reports must be published by September 2026
  • Reports may be produced in English or Bangla (with English translation if needed)
  • Applicants must include a risk management plan for safety
  • Applicants may include security‑related costs in the budget

Budget Requirements

The uploaded document states:

“We ask that the budgets be reasonable with a ceiling of 1500 EUR.” “Applications with smaller budgets will be more competitive.”

Budget guidelines:

  • Maximum budget: €1500
  • Must include a detailed Excel budget with justification
  • Should focus on reporting costs (travel, accommodation, translation, safety)
  • Should not focus on buying new equipment
  • Freelancers may include a stipend, but it must be no more than 30% of the total budget
  • Applicants must disclose other funding sources, if any

Judging Criteria

Applications will be evaluated on:

  • Relevance to climate change and the private sector
  • Originality and fresh angles
  • Reach of the publishing outlet
  • Impact potential
  • Innovative storytelling, including multimedia and data visualization
  • Feasibility and timely publication
  • Strength of the editor’s support letter

Application Process

To apply:

  1. Click Apply Now on the EJN website
  2. Log in or create an account
  3. Complete the application form
  4. Upload:
    • Detailed budget (Excel)
    • Two work samples
    • Signed editor support letter
  5. Submit before the deadline

 

Important Notes

  • Reports must include the tagline: “This story was produced with support from Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.”
  • EJN and partners retain rights to edit, publish, broadcast, and distribute the stories
  • Late applications will not be considered

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more  fellowship opportunities here 

The post Bangladesh Climate & Private Sector Reporting Fellowships 2026: A Complete Guide for Journalists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
